cited_work: Ten Arguments in Search of a Philosopher: Averroes and Aquinas in Ficino's Platonic Theology
relevant_topic: Averroes's actual repeated formula in the Long Commentary
claim_summary: Averroes's actual formula in the Long Commentary — 'neque est corpus neque virtus in corpore' (neither body nor power in a body) — is repeated again and again. This is the -P position (his claim against Alexander of Aphrodisias), not the -F position (denying the intellect is the substantial form of the body), which is a Thomistic paraphrase.
exact_quote: very close to the language of Averroes in the Long Commentary: that the Intellect, whether passive or active, neque est corpus neque virtus in corpore. Averroes repeats this phrase again and again.
notes: The Latin phrase is Averroes's own repeated formula in Michael Scot's translation of the Long Commentary.
